name: Other Research
description: Cross-cutting research: genetics, neuroimaging, pathophysiology, diagnostic criteria, and general headache medicine.

all_trials.title: Intranasal Civamide for Episodic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Intranasal civamide (zucapsaicin), a capsaicin-related compound, as a preventive nasal spray for episodic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with only 2 participants out of planned enrollment. Effectively failed to recruit.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A capsaicin-like nasal spray could theoretically desensitize the trigeminal nerve, but this study was far too small to tell us anything.

all_trials.title: Double-Blind Comparison of the Efficacy and Safety of C213 to Placebo for the Acute Treatment of Cluster Headaches
all_trials.what_tested: A zolmitriptan microneedle skin patch (C213) applied during acute cluster headache attacks versus placebo, at 1.9mg and 3.8mg doses.
all_trials.key_result: Pain relief at 15 minutes: placebo 3 patients, C213 1.9mg 5 patients, C213 3.8mg 5 patients. Sustained pain relief was similarly close across groups. With only 42 total participants and small numbers per group, no clear separation from placebo.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A needle-free triptan skin patch would be convenient, but this small study did not show a clear advantage over placebo for aborting CH attacks.

all_trials.title: Study Of Sumatriptan Succinate Injection Kit In Patients With Migraine or Cluster Headache In Japan
all_trials.what_tested: Self-injection of sumatriptan 3mg (half the standard Western dose) for aborting migraine and cluster headache attacks in Japan.
all_trials.key_result: 93.9% of cluster headache patients achieved headache relief at 30 minutes post-injection. This was a non-comparative study confirming self-injection feasibility in Japan.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Sumatriptan injection remains one of the most effective acute CH treatments -- even at the lower 3mg dose used in Japan, it works in over 90% of attacks.

all_trials.title: A Phase III Study of Civamide Nasal Solution (Zucapsaicin) for the Treatment of Episodic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Intranasal civamide (zucapsaicin) nasal solution versus placebo for treating episodic cluster headache over 49 days.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 60 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: The larger of two civamide studies for CH -- capsaicin-based nasal sprays remain an unproven but theoretically interesting approach.

all_trials.title: Nurse Intervention Trial
all_trials.sponsor: Norwegian University of Science and Technology
all_trials.what_tested: A nurse-led telephone follow-up program for headache patients starting preventive treatment, compared to standard GP follow-up.
all_trials.key_result: Recruiting 400 participants, expected completion December 2026.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Better follow-up could improve how well patients stick with their CH preventive medications -- not a drug trial but could improve care quality.

all_trials.title: A Close Examination of Patient Experiences in Cluster Headache Clinical Research
all_trials.what_tested: A survey studying barriers to clinical trial participation among cluster headache patients, particularly in underrepresented demographic groups.
all_trials.key_result: Not yet recruiting, planned for 500 participants.
all_trials.patient_relevance: CH trials often struggle to recruit enough patients -- understanding barriers could make future trials faster and more inclusive.

all_trials.title: Prevention and Acute Treatment of Chronic Cluster Headache Compared to Standard of Care
all_trials.what_tested: GammaCore (non-invasive vagus nerve stimulator) for both prevention and acute treatment of chronic cluster headache versus standard of care.
all_trials.key_result: GammaCore reduced CH attacks by 7.6 per week versus 2.0 per week with standard of care alone -- a significant improvement. This was one of the more positive CH results for the device.
all_trials.patient_relevance: GammaCore showed meaningful benefit for chronic CH prevention in this study -- about 5 fewer attacks per week compared to standard treatment alone.

all_trials.title: Prophylactic Treatment of Episodic Cluster Headache
all_trials.sponsor: Norwegian University of Science and Technology
all_trials.what_tested: Candesartan (an angiotensin receptor blocker blood pressure medication) as a preventive treatment for episodic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 40 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Candesartan has shown some promise for migraine prevention -- if it works for CH too, it would be a cheap, widely available option with fewer side effects than verapamil.

all_trials.title: Primary Care Clinician Commitments to Choosing Wisely®
all_trials.what_tested: Whether asking primary care doctors to commit to Choosing Wisely guidelines reduces unnecessary imaging tests for headaches.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 45 clinicians. This was about reducing unnecessary tests, not about CH treatment.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Not relevant to CH treatment -- this was about healthcare quality improvement for general headache management.

all_trials.title: Cluster Headache, Addictions and Vascular Function
all_trials.what_tested: Studying the relationship between cluster headache, addictive behaviors, and blood vessel function (endothelial function).
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 95 participants. Observational study exploring vascular and addiction links in CH.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Understanding vascular dysfunction in CH could explain why the condition runs in cycles and may help identify at-risk individuals.

all_trials.title: The Presence and Role of Zygomatic-temporal Neuroma Triggering Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Whether a neuroma (nerve tumor) of the zygomatic-temporal nerve could be triggering cluster headache, with surgical exploration and removal.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 5 participants. A highly exploratory study testing a novel theory about CH causation.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A very unconventional hypothesis -- if a specific nerve tumor triggers CH in some patients, surgery could cure them, but this is highly speculative.

all_trials.title: Osteopathic Manual Therapy (OMT) and Brain Structure and Function in Primary Headache Patients: A Pilot Study
all_trials.sponsor: Edward Via Virginia College of Osteopathic Medicine
all_trials.what_tested: Whether osteopathic manipulative therapy (OMT) changes brain structure and function in primary headache patients, measured by brain imaging.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 31 participants. This examined brain changes from manual therapy, not CH-specific.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Osteopathic manipulation is unlikely to treat CH specifically, but understanding how manual therapies affect the pain-processing brain could have broader implications.

all_trials.title: The Efficacy of L-cysteine in Prevention of Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: L-cysteine (sold as Acetium capsules) taken daily to prevent or abort cluster headache attacks, based on its ability to neutralize acetaldehyde.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 60 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: L-cysteine is an over-the-counter supplement -- if it works, it would be an accessible and cheap option for CH prevention, but we lack published evidence.

all_trials.title: An Open Label Study Using Consecutive Intravenous Depacon With Oral Depakote ER for the Treatment of Cluster Headaches.
all_trials.what_tested: IV Depacon (valproate) for 2-3 days followed by oral Depakote ER (extended-release valproate) to quickly break a cluster headache cycle.
all_trials.key_result: Average time to achieve 50% reduction in attack frequency or severity was 29.7 days (SD 13.8). This is not impressively fast for a treatment intended to provide rapid relief.
all_trials.patient_relevance: IV valproate followed by oral Depakote took about a month to halve attack burden -- this is not much faster than standard verapamil titration, limiting its practical advantage.
all_trials.dose_tested: 1000mg IV Depacon + 1000mg oral Depakote ER daily, then 500-2000mg Depakote ER maintenance

all_trials.title: Sleep in Cluster Headache: Sleep Parameters in- and Outside a Cluster Bout
all_trials.what_tested: Sleep study comparing sleep parameters in episodic cluster headache patients during bouts versus between bouts.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 66 participants. Observational sleep research.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Understanding how sleep differs during CH bouts could explain why attacks cluster at night and might lead to sleep-targeted treatments.

all_trials.title: Hydration in Children and Adolescents With Primary Headaches
all_trials.what_tested: Investigating the role of hydration and fluid intake in children and adolescents with primary headaches.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 60 participants. Pediatric study, not CH-specific.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Primarily relevant to pediatric headache -- dehydration is a known headache trigger but this is not specific to cluster headache.

all_trials.title: Personalised Process-based Psychological Interventions for Paediatric Headaches
all_trials.what_tested: Personalized psychological intervention (CBT and ACT-based) for adolescents with headaches, delivered in 4-5 online sessions.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 5 participants. A small single-case experimental design study for pediatric headache.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Psychological coping skills may help young headache patients but this tiny study is not specifically about CH.

all_trials.title: Intranasal Cooling for Cluster Headache and Migraine
all_trials.what_tested: Intranasal cooling (spraying a coolant into the nose to cool blood vessels supplying the brain) for aborting migraine and cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 15 participants (5 with CH). Each patient received 10 treatments of up to 20 minutes. No results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Cooling the blood vessels in the nose is a creative approach -- if it works, it would be drug-free and fast-acting, but results are unknown.
all_trials.dose_tested: Intranasal evaporative cooling, up to 20 minutes per treatment

all_trials.title: Pilot Study Evaluating the Use of Nasal Carbon Dioxide for the Treatment of Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Nasal carbon dioxide (CO2) delivered by a handheld device to abort cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated with 10 participants. Average pain reduction was -0.826 on a 0-3 scale (about 28% improvement). No serious adverse events. Study too small for conclusions.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Nasal CO2 showed modest pain reduction but the study was too small and had no placebo control -- not enough evidence to recommend it for CH.
all_trials.dose_tested: Nasal CO2 at 0.5 standard liters per minute, 10 seconds per nostril, up to 6 doses

all_trials.title: mBrain-21 : a Three-month Study on Context-awareness, Physiological Monitoring, and Machine Learning in Migraine and Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Three-month wearable sensor study using machine learning to predict headache attacks and track stress, sleep, and activity in migraine and cluster headache patients.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 30 participants. Technology development study.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Building toward smartwatch-based CH attack prediction -- could eventually enable preemptive treatment if the technology matures.

all_trials.title: Regional-anaesthesiological Infiltration Techniques for the Management of Chronic Pain: a Retrospective Study
all_trials.what_tested: Retrospective analysis of regional anesthetic infiltration techniques for chronic pain syndromes including refractory headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 83 participants. Retrospective data analysis of pain scores during treatment.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Local anesthetic infiltration is used by some pain clinics for refractory CH -- this retrospective data may add to the evidence base.

all_trials.title: Risk Taking and Decision-making in Cluster Headache Patients
all_trials.what_tested: Studying risk-taking and decision-making behavior in cluster headache patients to understand potential shared biology with addictive tendencies.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 140 participants. Behavioral/psychological research.
all_trials.patient_relevance: CH patients may have different reward-seeking behavior -- understanding this could shed light on shared hypothalamic pathways between CH and addiction.

all_trials.title: Circadian Rhythm and Attack-timing in Episodic Cluster Headache Patients in- and Outside of Bout: an Actigraphic Study
all_trials.what_tested: Using actigraphy (wrist activity monitors) to study circadian rhythm and attack timing in episodic cluster headache during and between bouts.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 60 participants. Published research confirmed the strong circadian pattern of CH attacks.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Confirmed what patients know: CH attacks follow the body clock -- this supports chronotherapy approaches and circadian-based treatments.

all_trials.title: Safety and Efficacy Study of SOM230 s.c. in Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: SOM230 (pasireotide), a somatostatin analogue, injected subcutaneously at 1.5mg to abort acute cluster headache attacks versus placebo.
all_trials.key_result: Headache response (pain dropping to mild/none at 30 min): 10 out of treated patients on SOM230 versus 9 on placebo -- essentially no difference. Study terminated.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Pasireotide did not work for aborting CH attacks -- it performed no better than placebo injection.

all_trials.title: Cluster Headache and SPINK-1 Gene
all_trials.what_tested: Whether a specific gene variant (SPINK-1) is associated with cluster headache, building on earlier genetic findings.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 42 participants. Genetic association study.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Finding genetic links to CH could eventually lead to targeted treatments or early identification of at-risk individuals.

all_trials.title: Variability of the Autonomic Nervous System (ANS) During Cluster Headache (CH).
all_trials.sponsor: Centre Hospitalier Universitaire de Saint Etienne
all_trials.what_tested: Studying autonomic nervous system variability during cluster headache attacks to determine if autonomic dysfunction triggers or results from attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated with 19 participants.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Understanding whether autonomic dysfunction causes or follows CH attacks is fundamental -- but this study was terminated before answering the question.

all_trials.title: Droperidol Versus Metoclopramide + Diphenhydramine for the Treatment of Primary Headaches
all_trials.what_tested: Comparing droperidol versus metoclopramide + diphenhydramine for emergency department treatment of primary headaches.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated with 19 participants. Results section exists but contains no actual pain score data. Not CH-specific.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Not specifically relevant to CH -- this was about ER headache treatment in general and was terminated early.
